g.text: FORMULA: P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B)

ul.li: P(A): Selectivity of Predicate 1 (Fraction of rows matching A)
P(B): Selectivity of Predicate 2 (Fraction of rows matching B)
P(A ∩ B): The Joint Probability (Probability both are true)

div.p: Calculating OR selectivity as a simple sum $(P(A) + P(B))$ results in over-estimation because the intersection is counted twice.

text.tspan: * @brief Estimates selectivity for OR predicates using Inclusion-Exclusion.
* @return combined selectivity corrected for independence overlap.
// Ensure result stays within valid probability bounds
* @brief Estimates selectivity for OR predicates using Inclusion-Exclusion.
* @return combined selectivity corrected for independence overlap.
// Ensure result stays within valid probability bounds

div.p: Implementation Note: This logic assumes statistical independence. In production optimizers, if Predicate A and Predicate B are correlated (e.g., make='Toyota' OR model='Camry'), the product $P(A) \times P(B)$ will under-estimate the overlap, leading to poor plans. In such cases, use Multi-Column Statistics or HyperLogLog sketches.
